column4: Lack of alignment in data structure. For example, the server returns an object (dictionary) while the client expects an Array.

(MoSCoW method)

column5: Relying on local SQLite databases results in fragmented and "stale" data across different developers' local machines.
column6:
Inconsistent testing
environments, leading
to integration failures
and masked bugs
During Frontend – Backend Synchronization.
column11:
Deploy a centralized
MySQL database to
establish a single
source of truth and
uniform real-time data
for the entire team.
